reason: The paper explicitly states "Importance reports the decrease in mean square error (MSE) for each feature" and Fig 5 caption says "Top 25 model features determined by variance of responses in Ranger" — this is impurity/variance (MDI-type) importance from the Ranger RF package, not permutation importance. The ranking is central to the Discussion's biological narrative: "the most important model feature...was the phylum Firmicutes," "EC appeared to be most influential," and these rankings are used to build causal/successional claims (the "Clostridium Effect," anaerobic vs. aerobic taxa dynamics, EC as a proxy for ammonium). The feature set spans wildly heterogeneous predictors (35-5195+ taxa of skewed compositional relative-abundance data mixed with continuous environmental measurements), exactly the setting where impurity importance is biased toward high-cardinality/skewed features. No permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or conditional importance is reported anywhere in the text (searches for "permutation," "impurity," "SHAP," "conditional," "cforest" all return zero hits besides the MSE-decrease description). The only "validation" present (S3 Table, train vs. test R²) assesses overall predictive accuracy, not the specific selected/ranked feature set, so it does not meet the adequate-corroboration bar. All three confirmation criteria are met.
